examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: no authentication, no TLS overrides, no derived fields. Only root.url needs to be filled in to get a working datasource pointed at a Loki server on localhost:3100.

basicAuth.description: Authenticate with a Loki that requires HTTP Basic. Both basicAuth and basicAuthUser live at the datasource root; only basicAuthPassword is secret.

noAuth.description: A public or network-isolated Loki with no HTTP-level auth. Maximum lines is bumped to 5000 for ad-hoc analysis and alerting UI management is enabled.

oauthForward.description: Forward the signed-in user's upstream OAuth identity to Loki. The editor writes only jsonData.oauthPassThru; there is no accompanying secret.

tlsMutualAuth.description: Loki requiring mTLS. jsonData.tlsAuth=true triggers the SDK to build a client-authenticated transport using serverName plus the PEM-encoded client cert and key in secureJsonData.

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

tlsSelfSignedCA.description: Loki behind a private CA. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true tells the SDK to verify the server certificate against the PEM-encoded CA in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.

withDerivedFields.description: Loki configured with a derived field that extracts a trace ID from the log line via regex and internally links to a Tempo data source. Two derived fields — one regex-based external link, one label-based internal link — demonstrate the shape.
